prompt: Help me set up a Jekyll docs site from scratch on macOS. About 40 pages, sidebar nav, light/dark mode. Ruby 3.4. I deploy via GitHub Actions to GitHub Pages. Need: a working Gemfile, a `_config.yml`, the deployment workflow, and an html-proofer step that breaks the build on broken internal links.

assertions.text: Installs Ruby via rbenv/asdf/mise — does NOT recommend system Ruby
Gemfile includes a docs-appropriate theme (e.g., just-the-docs) that supports sidebar nav and light/dark
Workflow gives `actions/configure-pages` step an `id: pages` so `steps.pages.outputs.base_path` resolves
Workflow sets `JEKYLL_ENV=production` on the build step
Workflow has an htmlproofer step that runs against `_site` and fails on broken internal links
htmlproofer step is scoped to internal links (e.g., `--disable-external`) to keep PR checks fast and stable
Uses current major versions: actions/checkout@v4, ruby/setup-ruby@v1, actions/configure-pages@v5, actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3, actions/deploy-pages@v4
Build step passes `--baseurl` from `${{ steps.pages.outputs.base_path }}` (or equivalent)
